summaries.history: The Samedi bloodline was created approximately 250 years ago when Antonio Giovanni's necromantic experiment went wrong. Attempting to transfer a human soul into a vampire in torpor, Antonio instead created the first Samedi—a Kindred trapped in a state of rapid decomposition. The first Samedi, known as 'Gran'daddy' or Paulo, escaped and began creating childer. The Giovanni hunted them, forcing them to flee to the Brazilian jungle via slave ships. The Samedi have since spread throughout the Americas, surviving by being useful as information brokers, assassins, and bodyguards.
summaries.culture: Samedi culture is built around survival, utility, and acceptance of their condition. They are solitary and independent, with only one Samedi per major city (unless there is a childe in training). They maintain connections through information networks and annual gatherings (Candomble) in Brazil and Louisiana. Samedi culture emphasizes practicality, resourcefulness, and defiance. They refuse to be ashamed of their condition, even as they acknowledge its horror. They are deeply connected to voodoo, spirit guides, and the world of the dead.
summaries.philosophy: Samedi believe that they are the physical embodiment of death and decay, and that all Kindred are dead—they just look the part better. They see their condition as a reminder of the reality of death, and they use this reality to their advantage. They believe in survival through utility, understanding that their continued existence depends on their value to others. They are proud of what they are, even as they acknowledge the horror of their condition.
summaries.factions: The Samedi are largely unstructured, with individual members operating independently. The main division is between those who attend the annual Candomble gatherings and those who do not. There is also a sub-group known as the Sons of Samuel, who are Tremere who have been Embraced by Samedi or who have learned Samedi Disciplines. The Samedi maintain connections through information networks, but they avoid large organizations or formal hierarchies.
summaries.politics: Samedi are largely apolitical, focusing on survival and utility rather than Kindred politics. They operate as information brokers, assassins, and bodyguards, selling their services to whoever can pay. They are tolerated (and sometimes protected) by those who find them useful, but they are hunted and destroyed by those who find them offensive. The Giovanni pay them hush money to stay away, and most other clans avoid them unless they need their services.
summaries.relations: Samedi have mixed relationships with other clans. They get along well with the Nosferatu, who share their outcast status and practical approach to survival. They are tolerated by the Tremere, who respect their knowledge and utility. They are avoided by most other clans, who find their appearance offensive. The Giovanni pay them to stay away, acknowledging their connection but wanting to keep it secret. The Samedi themselves are largely indifferent to other clans, viewing them as potential employers or threats.
summaries.character_creation: Samedi characters should be built around survival, utility, and acceptance of their condition. They typically emphasize Mental and Social Attributes, with Streetwise, Investigation, and Occult (for voodoo) as key Abilities. Their Disciplines include Thanatosis (their unique Discipline), Necromancy (from their Giovanni origins), and Obfuscate. Backgrounds often include Contacts (information networks), Resources (from their work), and Mentor (within the bloodline). Samedi characters should have a clear focus for their survival—information brokering, assassination, bodyguarding, or voodoo practice.
summaries.archetypes: Common archetypes include the information broker who collects secrets and sells them to the highest bidder; the assassin who uses their appearance and skills to eliminate targets; the bodyguard who uses voodoo and curses to protect their employers; the voodoo practitioner who maintains connections to the spirit world; and the outcast who has learned to embrace their condition and use it as a weapon. Archetypes often center on survival, utility, and the tension between isolation and connection.
summaries.storyteller_advice: Storytellers should use Samedi to provide information, create tension, and force characters to confront the reality of death and decay. They work best as information brokers, bodyguards, or specialists who are hired for their unique skills. Focus on their utility and their connection to voodoo and the spirit world. Remember that Samedi survive by being useful, so their actions should reflect this focus. Use them to explore themes of death, decay, survival, and the reality of unlife.

tone_highlights.emotional_temperature: Cold, morbid, and defiant. Emotions are channeled into survival and utility rather than displayed openly.
tone_highlights.social_posture: Direct, unapologetic, and practical. They speak plainly about death and decay, using dark humor to unsettle others.
tone_highlights.predator_style: They hunt with patience and precision, using their appearance and skills to their advantage. They are practical and efficient, using whatever means necessary.
recurring_motifs: Decay and rot as visible reality

common_archetypes.name: Information Broker
common_archetypes.description: A Samedi who collects secrets and information, selling them to the highest bidder and using knowledge as both currency and protection.

common_archetypes.name: Assassin
common_archetypes.description: A Samedi who uses their appearance and skills to eliminate targets, using explosives, curses, or direct violence as needed.

common_archetypes.name: Bodyguard
common_archetypes.description: A Samedi who uses voodoo, curses, and their appearance to protect their employers, understanding that their utility ensures their survival.

common_archetypes.name: Voodoo Practitioner
common_archetypes.description: A Samedi who maintains deep connections to voodoo, spirit guides, and the world of the dead, using this knowledge for power and protection.
